words.hint: Process plants use to make food from sunlight
words.educational: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy, producing glucose and oxygen from carbon dioxide and water.
Evolution is the process by which living organisms change over time through natural selection, leading to the diversity of life on Earth.
A molecule is formed when two or more atoms join together chemically. Water (H2O) and oxygen gas (O2) are examples of molecules.
Chromosomes are thread-like structures made of DNA that carry genetic information. Humans have 23 pairs of chromosomes.

words.hint: Community of living things and their environment
words.educational: An ecosystem includes all living organisms in an area and their physical environment, interacting as a system through energy flow and nutrient cycling.

words.hint: Neutral particle in an atom nucleus
words.educational: Neutrons are subatomic particles with no electric charge found in atomic nuclei. They help hold the nucleus together and determine isotopes.

words.hint: Substance that speeds up chemical reactions
words.educational: A catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed. Enzymes are biological catalysts.

words.hint: Large molecules essential for body structure
words.educational: Proteins are large biomolecules made of amino acids. They perform many functions including building tissues, catalyzing reactions, and fighting infections.
Velocity is the rate of change of an object's position. Unlike speed, velocity includes direction, making it a vector quantity.

words.hint: Energy transmitted through waves or particles
words.educational: Radiation is energy that travels through space as waves or particles. It includes visible light, radio waves, X-rays, and nuclear radiation.
